name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Belknap 09 Election
body:
**New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Belknap 09 Election** **For Immediate Release:** September 12, 2017**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 Concord – New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding the Belknap District 09 Election: "Special elections often favor the party out of power. The fact remains that under Republican leadership, 75% of voters agree New Hampshire is headed in the right direction. We remain confident Republicans will succeed in 2018 and beyond."

"I want to thank Steve Whalley for stepping up to the plate to run in this special election."
###

name: The White House
subject: Building Unity
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f6b92dc72a345703eeb6d27735bf7804bd7841c18b7ec2591ee4ec91d5fc85c3997c2c1ad1adc60d76a203084e3232b57cb9a6655ba339ff

President Donald J. Trump wants to continue building unity by working on more issues supported by both parties, especially restoring fairness to our broken tax code and cutting taxes for hardworking Americans. Last night, the President hosted a bipartisan dinner with Senators from both sides of the aisle at the White House. Today, he will host another bipartisan meeting at the White House.

-------------

Bipartisan Dinner with Senators

Last night, President Trump hosted Senators for a bipartisan dinner at the White House. Senators Joe Donnelly (D-IN), Orrin Hatch (R-UT), Joe Manchin (D-WV), John Thune (R-SD), Heidi Heitkamp (D-ND), and Pat Toomey (R-PA) came together to discuss advancing the Administration’s legislative priorities, in particular tax cuts for the middle class. The President asked the bipartisan group of Senators to help deliver tax cuts for American families, which is essential to economic growth and prosperity.

Hurricane Irma Relief Update

Yesterday, President Trump signed H.R. 3732, the "Emergency Aid to American Survivors of Hurricanes Irma and Jose Overseas Act, "a bill raising the cap for Fiscal Years (FY) 2017 and 2018 on the amount that HHS may spend for the provision of assistance to repatriated U.S. citizens from $1 million to $25 million.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f6b92dc72a3457031cc25722e7e5e4710736d9e0988fbc246cfd75a73ab1ea905ffdabad5e10214b0b497a63891a516d8d6c49defe39afa7
Read more

FEMA Briefing and Resources

Alex Amparo, Assistant Administrator for Recovery at the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) appeared in a live Facebook broadcast explaining actions that storm victims can take to receive assistance in the aftermath of Hurricane Irma. He urged citizens who have been displaced or sustained damages to their permanent residence to fill out their insurance claim, then register for assistance at
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f6b92dc72a345703d9fa8c6ac48a187f3b263ac53ee3ab07f84759786b7a6d8e73409f40b969879bd775a5669288379bbcb469a59fa03216
DisasterAssistance.gov .

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f6b92dc72a3457035cd17ce745b30a4b23779d583c915a7e92490be3a9b70a5f26d0eacb2f123469c17c0bf15124ee31ecaeb9bf50c9dfdf
Watch the video

Photo of the Day

President Trump welcomes Prime Minister Najib Abdul Razak of Malaysia to the White House | September 12, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Stephanie K. Chasez)

POTUS and VP TODAY

In the morning, President Trump will receive his daily intelligence briefing. The President and Vice President will then meet with the Domestic Policy Council. This afternoon, President Trump will be meeting with South Carolina Senator Tim Scott to discuss racial reconciliation. The President will continue meeting today with legislators from both sides of the aisle.

Later in the day, Vice President Pence will join Director of National Intelligence Dan Coats and Director of the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency Robert Cardillo to tour the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency and participate in a series of briefings.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, President Trump, First Lady Melania Trump, Vice Presdient Pence, and Mrs. Karen Pence will travel to Florida as part of Hurricane Irma’s recovery and relief efforts. On Friday, the President and First Lady will give remarks to members of the military at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland. Next week, President Trump and Vice President Pence will attend the United Nations General Assembly in New York.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f6b92dc72a345703ed11d3faf3612f0323213cf2c44da20d7c9daef9ba731d2186190787158ecd319fc768eb1c88caf92441a653710b4367
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f6b92dc72a345703ffc81434cd37fee854cbb54e1cf2a37a4c559f250559ead065c1672deba74c31d18e678d7f6aa942dbccb2023dc5b486
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4050348c57a06111ac7086c8a54c7a49f6b6335aa441711f23b9fd7521809d6b5ff74d1bca80bdbd13c53229a507b6ce2

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Some big news on our Medicare-for-all bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
Today, with 15 co-sponsors, I am officially introducing our Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care bill in the Senate.
#
Sisters and Brothers -
I have some important news to share with you:
**Today -- along with 15 co-sponsors -- I am officially introducing our Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care legislation that will guarantee health care as a right for every single man, woman and child in the United States of America.**
The last time I introduced this bill, it lacked a single co-sponsor.
Today, we have 15.
I want you to know the names of each and every Senator who is standing with us today, [**then sign my online card thanking them for joining this fight.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/mfa-senate-cosponsors?source=em170913-c "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/mfa-senate-cosponsors?source=em170913-c")
Here is the list:
Senator Tammy Baldwin (D-WI)
Senator Richard Blumenthal (D-CT)
Senator Cory Booker (D-NJ)
Senator Al Franken (D-MN)
Senator Kirsten Gillibrand (D-NY)
Senator Kamala Harris (D-CA)
Senator Martin Heinrich (D-NM)
Senator Mazie Hirono (D-HI)
Senator Patrick Leahy (D-VT)
Senator Ed Markey (D-MA)
Senator Jeff Merkley (D-OR)
Senator Brian Schatz (D-HI)
Senator Tom Udall (D-NM)
Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA)
Senator Sheldon Whitehouse (D-RI)
But it gets better.
Over the past two months, more than 500,000 people across the country have joined them as citizen co-sponsors of our bill. And in the House of Representatives, for the first time ever, a majority of the Democratic members have signed onto John Conyers's Medicare-for-all legislation.
There is no doubt about it, momentum is on our side.
It was not long ago that the idea of Medicare for all was dismissed and ridiculed by the corporate media and political establishment of this country. Today, a strong majority of Americans understand that it is the responsibility of the federal government to provide health care for all.
But we still have a long way to go.
Because the truth is, the insurance companies and drug companies in this country are extraordinarily powerful and they are not going down without a fight. They have obscene amounts of wealth and have used that money to protect a dysfunctional system that allows them to make billions in profits while leaving far too many Americans behind.
But in my view, the people of this country have had enough of the greed and recklessness of these industries.
We understand that it is a moral outrage that the United States is the only nation in the industrialized world that does not guarantee health care as a right to all of its people.
We believe it is a national emergency that 28 million of our sisters and brothers lack health care coverage and that many more are underinsured with high deductibles and copayments.
We are tired of going to the pharmacy to get medicine we need and the price triples for no other reason than because the drug companies now have the right to charge as much as they like. Unbelievably, one out of five adults under the age of 65 in this country who gets a prescription from a doctor cannot afford to get it filled.
The American people are sick and tired of a health care system that spends twice as much per capita as countries around the world but has worse outcomes. All while the insurance companies and the pharmaceutical industry reward their executives with outrageous compensation packages.
All of us get sick.
All of us need medicine.
All of us have accidents.
All of us need good quality health care.
**In my view, the function of a rational health care system in this country should be to provide quality care for everyone in a cost effective way, not to make health industry CEOs richer or drive up stock prices on Wall Street.**
Even with growing support in Congress, we are still taking on much of the political and financial establishment in this fight. But when we are united, when we stand together, we are more powerful than they could ever imagine.
Sisters and Brothers: In this pivotal moment in American history, let us lead our country forward to guarantee health care as a right and not a privilege. This is a struggle whose time has come. This is a struggle not just about health care but about the heart and soul of our country, about what we stand for as a people.
Please remember that when we stand together and do not let allow demagogues to divide us up by race, religion, national origin or sexual orientation, there is nothing that we cannot accomplish.
**That is the political revolution.**
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

Friend, the dysfunction in Washington is at an all-time high.

Despite the overwhelming message voters sent in 2016, DC politicians aren’t rolling up their sleeves and getting things done.

I understand that President Trump was elected because Americans were fed up with do-nothing politicians who sit on their hands, and neglect what Americans truly want in their elected leaders.

Plenty of time has passed, but still, we have no Obamacare repeal, no tax reform, and they haven't even started on building the wall. President Trump needs help from those who will not back down, who will be tenacious, who will achieve what they said they would.

I can promise you, fname, when I get to Washington, conservative change WILL happen. I will stand with President Trump to get Wisconsinites -- and all Americans -- the policies they voted for.

Friend - I created this STAND WITH TRUMP petition to let everyone know where I stand. I'm not afraid to stand up for what I believe in, and I'll never back down.

If you're with me -- and our President -- in wanting real conservative reform, **[I hope you'll sign it today.](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=7b367c145f1e4f0d993bb0f2afcc6a50&_e=pmkkdg6JMA_HJCMC3Z7t2id86-oV32qJf0b5TfLEsxcTJS1gMIHvNmat95qCUTGzplhWl-vO1cKJ6ljtOeZTnXEUN4X0-mC0UOVr_5m-rOBXFYBzezs-oxZbNOGh3eE_EOw4VaVCxqC24Wikghb1zM8mC-U9gddJPOQ6L8vxqlJbTfLU6bAvdY7riQKBmu064VV-sMoq14X3ixodD9f6gohSPu20tmiIb4CklXkEQEzYWM-t2JrK7A8nUOa9HqvL6JWkWj8uE1OW-lm8a_g_8RdP6Oblu0_568t-OkmHLISflrMkDM3qbEFJJ62bw9yU4ws1acmRJESZ3394Sf9d_NSL0gZ-wvs16ccl8xmnldugAYOPfgxRp4SkRrgsLsj-TRJ1sI0SUHVETYpxTaFNvA%3D%3D)**

clean_body: Despite the overwhelming message voters sent in 2016, DC politicians aren’t rolling up their sleeves and getting things done. I understand that...... Friend, the dysfunction in Washington is at an all-time high. Despite the overwhelming message voters sent in 2016, DC politicians aren’t rolling up their sleeves and getting things done. I understand that President Trump was elected because Americans were fed up with do-nothing politicians who sit on their hands, and neglect what Americans truly want in their elected leaders. Plenty of time has passed, but still, we have no Obamacare repeal, no tax reform, and they haven't even started on building the wall. President Trump needs help from those who will not back down, who will be tenacious, who will achieve what they said they would. I can promise you, fname, when I get to Washington, conservative change WILL happen. I will stand with President Trump to get Wisconsinites -- and all Americans -- the policies they voted for. Friend - I created this STAND WITH TRUMP petition to let everyone know where I stand. I'm not afraid to stand up for what I believe in, and I'll never back down. If you're with me -- and our President -- in wanting real conservative reform, Thank you, friend. Together, we can change Washington. Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: Medicare for All
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:
| | |
---
Friends -

Before he became president, Donald Trump promised "insurance for everybody." He promised coverage for more people at a lower cost. But so far all we've seen from him are devastating cuts that would strip tens of millions of people of their insurance.

**I'll work with any Republican who wants to improve our current health care system but let me be clear: Health care is not a privilege for the wealthiest few. It is a right for all. And we cannot continue to let partisan politics get in the way of that.**

That's why today I'm signing on with my colleagues to co-sponsor the Medicare for all bill, and I need you to stand with me:

**[Add your name to join me, Bernie Sanders and Americans across the country to support Medicare for all.](https://petitions.signforgood.com/medicareforall?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=cjs&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fcjs-medicare-for-all%3Fcode%3Djtk786-hnda)
**
We've made great progress increasing health care coverage, but the costs are still too high for far too many families. Let's speak out together for the millions of Americans who have to choose between taking a loved one to the doctor and paying the bills. Please join me and make your voice heard in support of Medicare for all:
**[
Join Democratic leaders and Americans across the country to support Medicare for all.](https://petitions.signforgood.com/medicareforall?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=cjs&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsurveys.signforgood.com%2Fcjs-medicare-for-all%3Fcode%3Djtk786-hnda)
**
Thank you,

Jeanne

name: Barbara Mikulski
subject: We could lose half the women of the Senate next year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
It's time to put on our lipstick, square up our shoulders, suit up, and fight. Ten of our Democratic Senate women are up for re-election next year -- and they need our support.
---
**[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20170913-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team --

It's time to put on our lipstick, square up our shoulders, suit up, and fight.

Ten of our Democratic Senate women are up for re-election next year -- that's nearly HALF the women of the Senate -- and they're counting on our support to win re-election and keep fighting the tough battles to stop Donald Trump's disastrous agenda.

[**Join me in standing up for our Democratic Senate women. Chip in $1 to the 2018 DSCC Women 's Senate Network to give our Senate women the resources they need to win.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20170913-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

As our country's longest-serving female Senator, I had to win a lot of re-election fights -- and the DSCC was with me every time. And now, today's Senate women are counting on us too.

When I first ran for Senate, it looked like an uphill battle. No woman had ever been elected to the Senate in her own right. Politics was for old guys in smoke-filled rooms who kept saying I didn't "look the part."

While we have more women in the Senate now, the nasty attacks haven't let up. And our Democratic women need our support now as much as ever before.

We elected a record-breaking number of women to the Senate in 2012. But women are not a fad, fancy or a year. Now that those women are up for re-election, it's up to us to send them back to the Senate to prove our women leaders are here to stay.

**Chip in $1 now to the 2018 DSCC Women 's Senate Network to help our Democratic Senate women fight and win the tough campaigns ahead:

[http://www.dscc.org/help-women-win ](http://action.dscc. Thank you,

Barbara Mikulski

name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Senator Shaheen's Support for Socialized Medicine
body:
**New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Senator Shaheen's Support for Socialized Medicine**
**For Immediate Release:** September 13, 2017**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 Concord – New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding Senator Shaheen signing onto Bernie Sanders' government takeover of health care: “Senator Shaheen’s endorsement of Bernie Sanders’ government healthcare plan is both a tacit admission that her ObamaCare debacle is a failure and at the same time a full embrace of unfiltered, 100-proof socialism. This model is failing in other countries and would add an unbearable burden on middle class taxpayers."
"While Governor Sununu is focusing on ways to make health insurance less expensive, our federal delegation led by Senator Shaheen is pushing out of touch policies. We need real solutions to lower health care costs, not government run programs that will cause costs to rise and families to suffer."
###
---

clean_body: New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Senator Shaheen's Support for Socialized Medicine For Immediate Release: September 13, 2017Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 Concord – New Hampshire GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding Senator Shaheen signing onto Bernie Sanders' government takeover of health care: “Senator Shaheen’s endorsement of Bernie Sanders’ government healthcare plan is both a tacit admission that her ObamaCare debacle is a failure and at the same time a full embrace of unfiltered, 100-proof socialism. This model is failing in other countries and would add an unbearable burden on middle class taxpayers." "While Governor Sununu is focusing on ways to make health insurance less expensive, our federal delegation led by Senator Shaheen is pushing out of touch policies. We need real solutions to lower health care costs, not government run programs that will cause costs to rise and families to suffer." | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire 10 Water Street Concord New Hampshire 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--

body:
How would you rate Betsy DeVos' performance as Education Secretary? |
---

| | | Peter,

**Back in February, Joe voted against confirming Betsy DeVos as Secretary of Education because he knew she'd be a disaster for our schools and our kids.**

But thanks to Senate Republicans, she was confirmed anyway -- and she's been as bad as we expected. She's proposed devastating cuts to our education system, tried to hand money meant for public schools over to private corporations, and worked to end programs set up to provide student loan debt relief.

**Now, she's even announced plans to roll back critical Title IX protections for victims of sexual assault.**

That's why we want to know how you think she's doing, Peter. We need 157 more responses to meet our goal of 500.

**How would you rate Betsy DeVos' performance as Education Secretary?**

name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: 50 Republicans who could lose >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c604e/24c69fd9/9fb2fb13/189aeb19/2347846152/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE922N_digital_email_20170913_OnNoticeExplainer_inact&source=WQEFE922N_digital_email_20170913_OnNoticeExplainer_inact")
---

Peter,

What does it mean to be one of the 50 Republicans that we put "On Notice"?

It means you've taken some bad, _bad_ votes -- like to pass Trumpcare and defund Planned Parenthood.

It means we know you represent a district or state a Democrat can win.

And it means we're determined to replace you with a pro-choice Democratic woman.

**[We've set up an On Notice Fund to support this work, and we're aiming to have $75,000 in it before the week is out-- donate $3 now to help reach our goal and defeat 50 On Notice Republicans.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c604e/24c69fd9/9fb2fb13/189aeb1a/2347846152/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170912_email_OnNoticeGoal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Weve+set+up+an+On+Notice+Fund+to+support&utm_campaign=WQEFE922N_digital_email_20170913_OnNoticeExplainer_inact&source=WQEFE922N_digital_email_20170913_OnNoticeExplainer_inact")**

We put a record number of Republicans On Notice this year (with more to come!), and it's because this is an unprecedented moment.

While President Trump is in the White House, turning Congress blue again is how we protect our rights. We need to flip control of at least one chamber in order to keep dangerous legislation off his desk.

So we're targeting Republicans we're confident we can defeat. We're already supporting seven women running against On Notice Republicans, and we're looking to recruit more to run against the other 43.

**Donate $3 to our On Notice Fund to make sure we're stocked up for the fights ahead:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/On-Notice-Fund](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c604e/24c69fd9/9fb2fb13/189aeb1b/2347846152/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170912_email_OnNoticeGoal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgOnNoticeFund&utm_campaign=WQEFE922N_digital_email_20170913_OnNoticeExplainer_inact&source=WQEFE922N_digital_email_20170913_OnNoticeExplainer_inact")**

Thank you -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List
